notes.subject: Math
notes.title: Basic Arithmetic
notes.content:
Arithmetic is the foundation of mathematics. Key operations include:
• Addition: Combining numbers to find the sum
• Subtraction: Finding the difference between numbers
• Multiplication: Repeated addition of a number
• Division: Breaking a number into equal parts

Example: 2 + 2 = 4, 5 × 3 = 15, 10 ÷ 2 = 5

notes.subject: Math
notes.title: Order of Operations
notes.content:
Remember PEMDAS/BODMAS:
• Parentheses/Brackets
• Exponents/Orders
• Multiplication and Division (left to right)
• Addition and Subtraction (left to right)

Example: 2 + 3 × 4 = 2 + 12 = 14 (not 20)

notes.subject: Science
notes.title: The Solar System
notes.content:
Our solar system consists of:
• The Sun: A star at the center
• 8 Planets: Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, Neptune
• Moons: Natural satellites orbiting planets
• Asteroids and Comets: Smaller celestial bodies

Earth is the third planet from the Sun and is the only known planet to support life.

notes.subject: Science
notes.title: States of Matter
notes.content:
Matter exists in three main states:
• Solid: Fixed shape and volume (ice)
• Liquid: Fixed volume but takes shape of container (water)
• Gas: No fixed shape or volume (water vapor)

Water transitions:
• Melting: Solid → Liquid (0°C)
• Boiling: Liquid → Gas (100°C)
• Freezing: Liquid → Solid (0°C)

notes.subject: History
notes.title: The Titanic Disaster
notes.content:
The RMS Titanic was a British passenger ship that sank on April 15, 1912.
• Collision: Hit an iceberg in the Atlantic Ocean
• Casualties: Approximately 1,500 people died
• Lifeboat Shortage: Not enough lifeboats for all passengers
• Legacy: Led to improved maritime safety regulations

It was considered 'unsinkable' but sank on its maiden voyage.

notes.subject: History
notes.title: World Capitals
notes.content:
Important world capitals to know:
• France - Paris: Known as the City of Light, famous for the Eiffel Tower
• United States - Washington D.C.: Capital city and seat of government
• United Kingdom - London: Historic capital on the Thames River
• Germany - Berlin: Capital and largest city
• Spain - Madrid: Capital in the center of Spain

Capitals are the administrative centers where government functions are based.
